pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 10, export_result.sh line 10, task description line 5
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 11, export_result.sh line 11, task description line 6
Code analysis: verifier.py line 55 uses 'if tri_count > 10000:' (strictly greater than, not greater than or equal)
Code analysis: verifier.py line 79 uses 'if mask_count >= 3:' for full workflow verification
Code analysis: setup_invesalius.sh line 73; HTTP verification confirmed file exists with 200 OK response
Task description line 2; Medical literature confirms 226 HU is the standard bone/soft-tissue threshold in CT imaging (Hounsfield Unit Chart reference)
Task description line 1; Medically reasonable for CT scan range

Code analysis: setup_task.sh references invesalius-launch; web search confirms InVesalius 3 is legitimate open-source medical imaging software from Brazilian CTI research institute, developed since 2001

privileged_info_summary: The task uses InVesalius 3, a legitimate open-source medical imaging software developed by the Brazilian CTI research institute. The CT Cranium DICOM dataset is a real sample downloaded from the official InVesalius GitHub releases (v3.0 release, 0051.zip file). The task requires performing boolean subtraction on segmentation masks with medically accurate Hounsfield Unit thresholds (bone threshold 226 HU is the standard in CT imaging). Output files are correctly specified as /home/ga/Documents/soft_tissue_only.stl and /home/ga/Documents/boolean_masks.inv3. The minimum mask count requirement (3) is correctly implemented in the verifier. However, the min_triangle_count metadata value of 10000 is inaccurate: the verifier uses strict greater-than comparison (> 10000), meaning the actual minimum for full credit is 10001, not 10000.
